title: Iteration Protocols, Generators, and Lazy Evaluation · Side-Effect Repair Bay
summary: Process records larger than memory while exposing malformed rows at a controlled boundary. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a bounded-memory lazy ingestion pipeline and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace iter, next, StopIteration, and single-pass iterator state exactly.
Explain generator suspension, local state retention, send points, and termination.
Measure memory and latency tradeoffs between eager and lazy transformation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-functional immutable transformation portfolio."""

from collections.abc import Callable, Iterable, Iterator
from functools import partial, reduce
from itertools import chain, islice
from typing import TypeVar

T = TypeVar('T')
U = TypeVar('U')

# Extend one pure, lazy, tested pipeline increment per activity.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record

Inspect the Iteration Protocol trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
